channel.title: Analog I/O on MPLAB Device Blocks for Simulink
channel.description: Recent content in Analog I/O on MPLAB Device Blocks for Simulink

item.title: MCHP DAC SAMx Documentation
item.description:
High-performance DAC for waveform generation and precision voltage output

When to use:

When to use:

  • SAM target device (SAME5x, SAME7x, SAMC2x families)
  • Analog voltage output needed (DC reference, waveform generation, control signal)
  • Eliminating external DAC chip (use internal 10-bit or 12-bit DAC)
  • Audio output generation (sine wave, arbitrary waveforms via DMA)
  • Differential signaling required (DAC0 - DAC1 mode, doubles output range)

When NOT to use:

  • dsPIC target — no internal DAC peripheral available

  • 12-bit resolution required — maximum 12-bit (SAME5x/E7x) or 10-bit (SAMC2x)

item.title: Analog Comparator Controller (ACC) SAMx
item.description:
Analog Comparator Controller (ACC) SAMx Block The ACC SAMx block configures the Analog Comparator Controller peripheral on SAM E70/S70/V71 devices. This block compares two analog signals and outputs a digital result, with optional DAC integration for programmable threshold comparison.

When to use:

When to use:

  • SAM E7x/S7x/V7x target device selected (SAME70, SAMS70, SAMV71 families)
  • Fast voltage threshold detection needed (overcurrent, overvoltage, zero-crossing)
  • Programmable threshold via internal DAC (eliminate external comparator circuit)
  • PWM fault generation from analog condition (cycle-by-cycle current limit)
  • Hysteresis required for noisy signals (25 mV or 45 mV options)

When NOT to use:
